search_queries.query: ("Xpert Ultra" OR "MTB/RIF Ultra" OR "GeneXpert Ultra") AND ("sensitivity" OR "specificity" OR "diagnostic accuracy") AND humans[Filter]
"Xpert Ultra" AND "sputum" AND "culture" AND "pulmonary" NOT "review"[Publication Type] NOT "meta-analysis"[Publication Type]

abstracts_used_for_back_compute.title: Xpert MTB/RIF Ultra for detection of Mycobacterium tuberculosis and rifampicin resistance: a prospective multicentre diagnostic accuracy study
abstracts_used_for_back_compute.population: Adults with pulmonary TB symptoms (case detection group)
abstracts_used_for_back_compute.countries: South Africa, Uganda, Kenya, India, China, Georgia, Belarus, Brazil
abstracts_used_for_back_compute.raw_quote: Sensitivities of Xpert Ultra and Xpert were ... 88% and 83%, respectively, across all 462 participants with culture-positive sputum ... Specificities of Xpert Ultra and Xpert for case detection were 96% and 98% (-2.7%, -3.9 to -1.7) overall ... 1753 participants were distributed to the case detection group (n=1439) and the multidrug-resistance risk group (n=314).

abstracts_used_for_back_compute.title: The transition to Xpert MTB/RIF ultra: diagnostic accuracy for pulmonary tuberculosis in Kampala, Uganda
abstracts_used_for_back_compute.raw_quote: Among 698 participants included, 211 (30%) were HIV-positive and 336 (48%) had TB. The sensitivity of Ultra was 90.5% (95% CI 86.8-93.4) and specificity was 98.1% (95% CI 96.1-99.2).

abstracts_used_for_back_compute.title: Clinical Characteristics of Pulmonary Tuberculosis in Children Tested by Xpert MTB/RIF Ultra
abstracts_used_for_back_compute.raw_quote: The sensitivity and specificity of Xpert Ultra were 45.0% (149/331) and 96.7% (58/60), respectively.

abstracts_used_for_back_compute.title: Diagnostic contribution of GeneXpert Ultra in pediatric pulmonary tuberculosis
abstracts_used_for_back_compute.raw_quote: A total of 41 patients were included, of whom 63% (26/41) were diagnosed with PTB. ... The sensitivity and specificity of Ultra were 50% (13/26) and 100% (15/15), respectively.

abstracts_used_for_back_compute.title: Xpert Ultra Assay on Stool to Diagnose Pulmonary Tuberculosis in Children
abstracts_used_for_back_compute.raw_quote: Of 447 children, 29 (6.5%) were bacteriologically confirmed on induced sputum and 72 (16.1%) were bacteriologically confirmed. With "bacteriologically confirmed on induced sputum" as a reference, the sensitivity and specificity of Xpert Ultra on stool were 58.6% and 88.1%, respectively.

abstracts_excluded_or_not_extracted.reason: Cochrane meta-analysis of extrapulmonary TB — not pulmonary; pooled-meta-data not appropriate as a primary-research row in our DTA pool
TB-EASY tongue-swab assay (different test); Xpert is comparator only
Truenat Cochrane review — Xpert Ultra is a comparator with pooled meta-data only, not individual-study counts
Sung 2024 — community screening of trace results, not a primary DTA design with N_pos/N_neg cleanly extractable
Yenew 2024 — abstract gives Sens% but not unambiguous N_pos/N_neg breakdown for back-compute
Slail 2023 — extrapulmonary primary, no N+/N- breakdown in abstract

summary: 5 trials extracted (k=5 combined). Primary tier (CT.gov-linked publications): k=1 (Dorman 2018). Sensitivity tier (PubMed-abstract back-computed only): k=4. Per spec §5 acceptance gate, this triggers ship-with-coverage_warning banner (primary k<5). Combined k=5 ≥ 5 floor, < 8 ceiling — the review will display the coverage_warning prominently and the engine will route through k<10 Wald-CI flag.
